connectorUiConfig.title: Cisco Meraki Events (using REST API) (via Codeless Connector Framework)
connectorUiConfig.descriptionMarkdown:
The [Cisco Meraki](https://aka.ms/ciscomeraki) connector allows you to easily connect your Cisco Meraki organization events (Security events, Configuration Changes and API Requests) to Microsoft Sentinel. The data connector uses the [Cisco Meraki REST API](https://developer.cisco.com/meraki/api-v1/#!get-organization-appliance-security-events) to fetch logs and supports DCR-based [ingestion time transformations](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/logs/custom-logs-overview) that parses the received data and ingests into ASIM and custom tables in your Log Analytics workspace. This data connector benefits from capabilities such as DCR based ingestion-time filtering, data normalization.

In addition to the ASIM-normalized events, this connector also ingests Cisco Meraki Dashboard inventory and wireless security data into custom tables - Organizations, Network Clients, Organization Networks, and wireless Air Marshal (rogue access point) events.

 **Supported ASIM schema:** 
 1. Network Session 
 2. Web Session  
 3. Audit Event

graphQueries.metricName: Total Air Marshal Event logs received
graphQueries.legend: Cisco Meraki Air Marshal Event Logs

graphQueries.baseQuery: ASimWebSessionLogs | where EventProduct == 'Meraki' and EventVendor == 'Cisco' and EventOriginalType == 'API Request'

sampleQueries.description: Get sample of Cisco Meraki Organization logs
Get sample of Cisco Meraki Network Client logs
Get sample of Cisco Meraki Organization Network logs
Get sample of Cisco Meraki Air Marshal Event logs

sampleQueries.query:
CiscoMerakiOrganizations
 | extend CloudInfo = parse_json(Cloud)
 | extend RegionName = tostring(CloudInfo.region.name)
 | summarize Count=count() by RegionName
 | order by Count desc

CiscoMerakiOrganizations
 | extend LicensingInfo = parse_json(Licensing)
 | extend LicensingModel = tostring(LicensingInfo.model)
 | summarize Count=count() by LicensingModel
 | order by Count desc

sampleQueries.description: Get online network clients from last 24 hours
sampleQueries.query:
CiscoMerakiNetworkClients
 | where TimeGenerated > ago(24h) and Status == 'Online'
 | project TimeGenerated, MacAddress, IpAddress, Description, OperatingSystem, Ssid
 | order by TimeGenerated desc

CiscoMerakiOrganizationNetworks
 | extend ProductTypesList = parse_json(ProductTypes)
 | mvexpand ProductTypesList
 | summarize Count=count() by tostring(ProductTypesList)
 | order by Count desc

sampleQueries.description: Get rogue access points detected in last 7 days
sampleQueries.query:
CiscoMerakiAirMarshalEvents
 | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
 | project TimeGenerated, Ssid, Channels, FirstSeen, LastSeen, WiredMacs
 | order by TimeGenerated desc

sampleQueries.description: Get IDS alerts from last 24 hours
sampleQueries.query:
ASimNetworkSessionLogs | where EventProduct == 'Meraki' and EventVendor == 'Cisco' and EventType == 'Notable'
 | where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
 | project TimeGenerated, EventMessage, SrcIpAddr, DstIpAddr, ThreatName, EventSeverity
 | order by TimeGenerated desc

sampleQueries.description: Get configuration changes from last 24 hours
sampleQueries.query:
ASimAuditEventLogs | where EventProduct == 'Meraki' and EventVendor == 'Cisco'
 | where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
 | project TimeGenerated, ActorUsername, Operation, Object, OldValue, NewValue, EventType
 | order by TimeGenerated desc

dataTypes.name: CiscoMerakiOrganizations
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery:
CiscoMerakiOrganizations
 | where TimeGenerated > ago(12h) | summarize Time = max(TimeGenerated)
 | where isnotempty(Time)

dataTypes.name: CiscoMerakiNetworkClients
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery:
CiscoMerakiNetworkClients
 | where TimeGenerated > ago(12h) | summarize Time = max(TimeGenerated)
 | where isnotempty(Time)

dataTypes.name: CiscoMerakiOrganizationNetworks
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery:
CiscoMerakiOrganizationNetworks
 | where TimeGenerated > ago(12h) | summarize Time = max(TimeGenerated)
 | where isnotempty(Time)

dataTypes.name: CiscoMerakiAirMarshalEvents
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery:
CiscoMerakiAirMarshalEvents
 | where TimeGenerated > ago(12h) | summarize Time = max(TimeGenerated)
 | where isnotempty(Time)

dataTypes.name: ASimWebSessionLogs
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery:
ASimWebSessionLogs | where EventProduct == 'Meraki' and EventVendor == 'Cisco'
 | where TimeGenerated > ago(12h) | summarize Time = max(TimeGenerated)
 | where isnotempty(Time)

dataTypes.name: ASimAuditEventLogs
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery:
ASimAuditEventLogs | where EventProduct == 'Meraki' and EventVendor == 'Cisco'
 | where TimeGenerated > ago(12h) | summarize Time = max(TimeGenerated)
 | where isnotempty(Time)

dataTypes.name: ASimNetworkSessionLogs
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery:
ASimNetworkSessionLogs | where EventProduct == 'Meraki' and EventVendor == 'Cisco'
 | where TimeGenerated > ago(12h) | summarize Time = max(TimeGenerated)
 | where isnotempty(Time)

resourceProvider.permissionsDisplayText: Read and Write permissions are required.

instructionSteps.title: Configuration steps for the Cisco Meraki Dashboard API
instructionSteps.description: Follow the instructions below to obtain your Cisco Meraki API credentials.

parameters.content:
#### 1. Sign in to Meraki Dashboard
Navigate to [Cisco Meraki Dashboard](https://dashboard.meraki.com) and sign in with your administrator account.

#### 2. Generate API Key
1. Navigate to **Organization > API & Webhooks** from the left navigation menu
2. Select **API keys and access** from the top tabs
3. Click **Generate new API key** or use an existing one
4. Copy the API key and store it securely (it will only be shown once)

#### 3. Retrieve Organization ID
1. While logged into the Meraki Dashboard, look at the URL in your browser
2. The Organization ID appears in the URL as `https://dashboard.meraki.com/o/<organizationId>/...`
3. Copy the Organization ID for use below

#### 4. API Access Requirements
- The API key must have **Read** access to:
  - Organization settings
  - Network settings
  - Wireless settings
- Rate limit: 10 requests per second per organization
- Maximum lookback period: 31 days

instructionSteps.title: Connect Cisco Meraki organizations to Microsoft Sentinel
instructionSteps.description: This connector supports multi-tenant ingestion. Add one connection per Cisco Meraki organization; each connection ingests events for that organization in parallel. Use the grid below to review existing connections or add a new one.
